title: Testing with vi.hoisted for top-of-module mocks
scenario_context: vi.mock factory accesses outside variable, fails because mock hoisted above declaration.
what_should_be_tested: That mock setup works even when referencing test-scoped values.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Closure over uninitialized var in vi.mock
test_strategy_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. vi.mock is hoisted to top of file. References to non-hoisted vars are undefined at evaluation.
2. vi.hoisted lets you hoist values alongside.
3. Pattern: define mock implementations via vi.hoisted, reference them in vi.mock.
mock_strategy: For test-121, Testing with vi.hoisted for top-of-module mocks, mock only nondeterministic clocks, random ids, or external network providers. Keep the real vitest boundary, serializers, auth/session shape, and the assertion target visible: That mock setup works even when referencing test-scoped values.. Deterministic seed 121 reproduces failures.
what_would_break_without_this_test: Mocks reference undefined at evaluation; ReferenceError.
ci_considerations: CI for Testing with vi.hoisted for top-of-module mocks runs with deterministic seed 121, isolated fixture namespace, captured logs/artifacts on failure, max runtime budget tied to vitest, and zero hidden network or wall-clock dependency. Flake policy: one retry only for infrastructure-class failures, then quarantine with owner and failing seed.

title: Test artifacts and trace upload
scenario_context: CI fails; developer can't reproduce. No artifacts uploaded.
what_should_be_tested: On failure, traces/screenshots/videos/logs uploaded to CI artifacts.
test_strategy_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Conditional upload on failure.
2. 14-day retention for debugging windows.
3. Combine playwright-report + vitest logs.
solution:
/*
      - name: Upload Playwright artifacts
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-${{ github.run_id }}
          path: |
            playwright-report/
            test-results/
          retention-days: 14
      - name: Upload coverage
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-${{ github.run_id }}
          path: coverage/
          retention-days: 7
*/
mock_strategy: For test-147, Test artifacts and trace upload, mock only nondeterministic clocks, random ids, or external network providers. Keep the real infrastructure boundary, serializers, auth/session shape, and the assertion target visible: On failure, traces/screenshots/videos/logs uploaded to CI artifacts.. title: Testing what NOT to break (contract pinning)
scenario_context: Public API stable. Refactor accidentally changes response shape; clients break.
what_should_be_tested: Public API shape pinned via snapshot/schema; intentional changes require deliberate update.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Easy snapshot updates without version bump
test_strategy_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Public API responses tested with frozen schema.
2. Schema change = major version + migration notice.
3. Versioning per route.

Keep the real test strategy boundary, serializers, auth/session shape, and the assertion target visible: Public API shape pinned via snapshot/schema; intentional changes require deliberate update.. Deterministic seed 150 reproduces failures.

Flake policy: one retry only for infrastructure-class failures, then quarantine with owner and failing seed.
